result.markdown: --- title: "[integrated_auc()]{.doc-object-name .doc-function .doc-label .doc-label-function}" body-classes: doc-api-page page-navigation: false html-table-processing: none --- ::: {.doc-subject} Time-averaged IPCW AUC across multiple time points. ::: ::: {.doc-usage-source} Usage [Source](https://github.com/rich-iannone/greenwood/blob/main/greenwood/_metrics.py#L679-L743){target="_blank" rel="noopener"} ::: ::: {.doc-signature .doc-Kind.FUNCTION} ```python integrated_auc(surv, marker, times) ``` ::: ::: {.doc-text} Summarises `time_dependent_auc()` into a single number via trapezoidal integration over the supplied time range. This provides a discrimination summary analogous to Harrell's C-statistic but with explicit IPCW bias-correction for censoring. **Interpretation**: Same scale as `time_dependent_auc()` (0.5 = random, 1.0 = perfect). Values of 0.6-0.7 indicate moderate and 0.7+ indicate strong discrimination. ::: ## Parameters {.doc-parameters} ::: {.doc-definition-items} [surv]{.doc-parameter-name}[:]{.doc-parameter-annotation-sep} [Surv]{.doc-parameter-annotation} : A right-censored `Surv` response. [marker]{.doc-parameter-name}[:]{.doc-parameter-annotation-sep} [Any]{.doc-parameter-annotation} : Risk score for each subject. Higher values indicate higher risk. [times]{.doc-parameter-name}[:]{.doc-parameter-annotation-sep} [Any]{.doc-parameter-annotation} : Evaluation times (at least 2). The integrated AUC is computed as the area under the AUC curve from `times[0]` to `times[-1]`, normalized by the time span. `nan` time points (no cases or controls) are dropped before integration. ::: ## Returns {.doc-returns} ::: {.doc-definition-items} [float]{.doc-parameter-annotation} : Time-averaged AUC in [0, 1]. Higher is better. ::: ## Details {.doc-details} The integrated AUC is computed as the area under the time-dependent AUC curve (from `time_dependent_auc()`) divided by the time span, giving a single scalar summary of discrimination across the specified horizon. Time points that produce `nan` AUC values (no cases or no controls at that time) are dropped before integration. ## Examples {.doc-examples} Compute the integrated AUC of a Cox model's linear predictor across three clinically relevant time horizons (6, 12, and 18 months): ::: {#848ec8f5 .cell execution_count=1} ``` {.python .cell-code} import greenwood as gw # Load data and build a right-censored response lung = gw.load_dataset("lung", backend="polars") y = gw.Surv.right(lung["time"], event=(lung["status"] == 2)) cox = gw.CoxPH().fit(y, lung[["age", "sex"]]) # Compute the time-averaged AUC across three horizons lp = cox.predict(type="lp") gw.integrated_auc(y, lp, times=[180, 365, 540]) ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-display execution_count=1} ``` 0.6084157733720682 ``` ::: :::
